author: Whitney Winters
review: Porchlight does not "foster good relationships" landlords have zero incentive to provide professional or ethical business. I wouldn't bother with their move out checklist they will take your deposit anyways.

ownerResponse.text: Im sorry you dont feel your move-out charges were reasonable, but I assure you there were no funds left to replenish our peroxide or self-tanner reserves . Pets are great, but when you move into a brand new unit with a dog, you can expect some consequences. I have photos showing the soil extracted from the flooring, and the odor is still perceptible even though we had the only carpets completely replaced ($1187). Some blinds ($57), trim ($397) and drywall ($250) were also damaged, and needed to be replaced. Im not able to attach photos to this reply, but Id be happy to send them to you. There was also extensive cleaning ($380), and the unpleasant task of removing the bidet ($60). You also left your final water bill unpaid ($300+), which resulted in shutoff for the new tenants, but we appreciate that you promptly repaid us upon notification. We did let you out of your lease a month early, and didnt charge you the early termination fee of $1125 that was specified in your lease. We strive to have great relationships with our owners and tenants, and generally do until we have to hold tenants responsible for damage that they or their pets have caused.

author: deedee zarate
review: I hate writing bad reviews but this couldn't go unnoticed. We were excited to move into the La Villa apartments and at first the staff was really helpful and nice. As time progressed, we had a few problems with our upstairs neighbors (a

ownerResponse.text:
We're disappointed to hear you didn't have a great experience with Porchlight. We try to foster good relationships, but always recommend that tenants reach out to law enforcement when they have problems with neighboring tenants or feel threatened. We don't have cameras on a lot of our properties, so it's not always possible to resolve issues when individuals have differing perspectives of events. 
We make it a general rule never to comment on the cleanliness of a unit prior to keys being turned in, because a unit can appear clean on the surface, but actually still need quite a few hours of work.
We recently acquired a new full-time Maintenance Coordinator, so any deficiencies in communications regarding repairs and maintenance have been improved upon!  We're sorry La Villa didn't work out for you long term, but wish you the best.

review: I had applied for a commercial unit porch light was responsible for. After a month of emailing back and forth,  I was informed that I had been approved for the unit and that they would write up the lease for it.  And since i had been

author: cory daniel wolf
review: I sure appreciate the management team at Porchlight Property Management, they are always very pleasant and helpful if I have any problems!

author: C.A. Carebear Starr
review: Wonderful experience with the different staff members that I've been able to work with.  Marlae is great at what she does,  but is also a STELLAR HUMAN BEING. The entire experience & time in which I was living in a rental managed by

author: Vitaliy Shtempel
review: Pure scam - showed me 2 apartments, after coming and seen those apartments, she told me that they are NOT AVAILABLE right away during showings, because someone already applied and been approved. Charged 40$ TWICE for showing UNAVAILABLE TO

ownerResponse.text: Hi Vitaliy, I'm sorry you had a negative experience with our company. Properties have been renting very quickly lately, and we're not always immediately aware when someone applies online for one of our units. Also, documents we requested from you to support your applications were not provided promptly, which left the opportunity for others to apply. We only charge one application fee, per adult, regardless of how many showings. You applied with another adult, so you were each charged one application fee. After receiving the requested information, our staff person called to offer you a different unit, and was told you had found a different property to rent. Based on your treatment of our staff, we don't regret our hesitancy in approving your applications.

author: Jeff Pygott
review:
I would not use them again.
Lots of tenant damage, and they didn't do a walk through before the tenants left.

ownerResponse.text:
Our policy is to conduct a final walk-through after the tenants turn in keys. In this case, the tenants dropped the keys through our night slot over a weekend, and by Monday morning, the owners had moved back into the property (without notifying us of their return date). The home was 20+ years old, and had been occupied by the same tenants for 7+ years, and there was undeniably some wear and tear. The home was also quite dated, and the owners had told us they would be remodeling prior to moving back in, so its possible we weren't as diligent near the end of the tenancy (since there had been no obvious damage during any of our visits).The tenants had a mentally disabled child (who grew to maturity) while residing there, as well as an emotional support animal, and there was some pretty significant damage to walls, trim, cabinets, flooring, etc. We sent a contractor to the home to provide an estimate for repairs, but he was turned away. We told the owners we needed to send the tenant a statement, or refund of their deposit within 21 days, but they didn't send their estimated charges until six days after the deadline. Prior to that, we assessed the damages (based on our standardized list of charges), sent the tenant a statement and retained their deposit. We billed the tenant, several thousand dollars, which we believe they would've eventually paid, had the owner not taken legal action. We were legally not permitted to send them to collections, due to Covid-19 restrictions. The owner is pursuing payment of an amount we believe is equivalent to the cost of a significant remodel.
Our creed has always been "Adhering to the Golden Rule to provide the gold standard in property management", and we strive to always treat our people (and our properties) as we would want to be treated. We are disappointed when someone isn't happy with our service, but fortunately that is a rare occurrence!

author: Yevgeniy Zadneprovskiy
review: Best Property Management company weve worked with. They were very professional, response times were quick and when we had a couple issues come up at our rental we had resolution quickly. Both Mitzi and Marlae were awesome and wed happily work with them again.

review: It's just my personal experience, but when they go and start making false accusations about my dog biting people that is going to far.

author: Derek Schranz
review: These property managers are not professional by any means!!  They wont fix things, wont correspond with you, talk down to you and treat people unfairly!!   I would not recommend renting from them at all!!!  STAY FAR FAR AWAY FROM THEM OR THEIR BUSINESSES!!

author: 1One Love
review: They lied about renting a unit to me and tried to take my deposit

ownerResponse.text: Since this person provided vague 1-star reviews for several property management firms at nearly the same time, it appears to be an unfounded negative campaign against property managers in general. We only take a deposit after someone is approved for a unit, which holds the unit for up to 10 days, and is applied to first month's rent.
